phrases.intent_en: Ask if this is the birthplace of composer Erik Satie
phrases.text_local: Est-ce bien la maison natale du compositeur Erik Satie ?
phrases.pronunciation: ess kuh byahn lah may-ZOHN nah-TAL doo kohm-poh-zee-TUR ay-reek sah-TEE
phrases.cultural_note: Erik Satie (1866–1925), composer of the Gymnopédies and Gnossiennes, was born in Honfleur. The Maisons Satie are a whimsical, immersive museum occupying his birth house, designed to evoke his eccentric and playful personality.

phrases.intent_en: Ask how much the entry ticket costs
phrases.cultural_note: The Maisons Satie is a modest museum with an immersive audio experience celebrating the composer Erik Satie.

phrases.intent_en: Ask how long the visit takes
phrases.text_local: Combien de temps dure la visite des Maisons Satie ?
phrases.pronunciation: kohm-BYAHN duh tahn DOOR lah vee-ZEET day may-ZOHN sah-TEE
phrases.cultural_note: The self-guided audio-visual tour takes approximately 45 minutes to an hour. There is no prescribed route — you move at your own pace through the surreal installations. It is one of Honfleur's most original cultural experiences.

phrases.intent_en: Buy a CD of Satie's music in the museum shop
phrases.text_local: Est-ce qu'il y a des enregistrements de la musique de Satie à la boutique ?
phrases.pronunciation: ess keel ee ah day ahn-ruh-zhees-TRUH-mahn duh lah moo-ZEEK duh sah-TEE ah lah boo-TEEK
phrases.cultural_note: The museum shop stocks recordings of Satie's piano music, books about his life, and quirky Satie-themed gifts. His Gymnopédies remain among the most recognised piano pieces in Western music.

responses.phonetic: wee, ah lah boo-TEEK, ahn BAH
responses.french: Oui, à la boutique, en bas.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if photography is allowed inside
phrases.text_local: Est-ce qu'on peut prendre des photos à l'intérieur du musée ?
phrases.pronunciation: ess kohn PUH PRAHN-druh day foh-TOH ah lahn-tay-RYUR doo moo-ZAY
phrases.cultural_note: Photography policies vary room by room. The theatrical nature of the installations means some spaces have dim lighting and the headphone experience is more immersive if you focus on listening rather than photographing.
